title: NA
text: (functions optimized agentic use, can called directly without arguments.)
code: # Executing code devtools::load_all() code  # Tests devtools::test() # all tests devtools::test(filter = "^{name}") # tests for files starting with {name} devtools::test_active_file("R/{name}.R") # tests for R/{name}.R devtools::test_active_file("R/{name}.R", desc = 'blah') # single test with exact description "blah" (no regexp)  # Test coverage devtools::test_coverage() # all files devtools::test_coverage_active_file("R/{name}.R") # coverage for R/{name}.R from tests in tests/testthat/test-{name}.R  # Documentation devtools::document() # redocument package pkgdown::check_pkgdown() # check website  # Run complete R CMD check devtools::check()

title: NA
text: three possible ways run code, listed rough order desirability: ’re running inside Posit Assistant otherwise executeCode() tool available, use run code session user can also interact . Otherwise, R REPL (e.g. mcp__r__repl btw::run_r) available, use . Note mcp__r__repl uses sandbox blocks network requests reads/writes outside current directory. Otherwise, use Rscript -e "code".

title: NA
text: Follow tidyverse style guide Always run air format . generating code. Use base pipe operator (|>), magrittr pipe (%>%). Use \() ... single-line anonymous functions. cases, use function() {...}.

title: NA
text: Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests. Strive keep tests minimal comments. Never put code test-{name}.R file outside test_that() block. Instead, use tests/testthat/helper.R tests/testthat/helper-{name}.R. Avoid expect_true() expect_false() favor specific expectation better failure message. expectations newer releases might know expect_all_true(), expect_all_equal(), expect_r6_class(). use expect_error() expect_warning() error warning known class. Generally, prefer expect_snapshot(error = TRUE) errors expect_snapshot() warnings allow user review full text output. Avoid .package argument local_mocked_bindings(); modifies namespace another package, good practice. Instead create mockable version function current package. See ?local_mocked_bindings details.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing function exported roxygen2 documentation. Internal functions roxygen documentation. Wrap roxygen2 comments 80 characters. Whenever add new (non-internal) documentation topic, also add topic _pkgdown.yml. Always re-document package changing roxygen2 comment. Use pkgdown::check_pkgdown() check topics included reference index.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing change given bullet NEWS.md. Small documentation changes. Internal refactorings. Fixes bugs introduced current dev version. bullet briefly describe change end user mention related issue parentheses. bullet can consist multiple sentences contain newlines (.e. line wrap). change related function, put name function early bullet. change related issue, include issue number parentheses. include GitHub username PR created someone isn’t author. Order bullets alphabetically function name. Put bullets don’t mention function names beginning.

title: NA
text: need deprecate function argument? Read output usethis::learn_tidy_skill("deprecate"). adding input checking existing function writing new exported function? Read output usethis::learn_tidy_skill("arg-checking").

title: NA
text: user asks commit, use markdown commit message, don’t line wrap. commit fixes issue, include Fixes #num. line. push user explicitly requests .

title: NA
text: Use sentence case headings. Use US English.

title: NA
text: user asks proofread file, act expert proofreader editor deep understanding clear, engaging, well-structured writing. Work paragraph paragraph, always starting making TODO list includes individual items top-level section. Fix spelling, grammar, minor problems without asking user. Label unclear, confusing, ambiguous sentences FIXME comment. report changed.

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: James (2026). graft: Table-Native Knowledge Layer. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://jameshwade.github.io/graft/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {graft: A Table-Native Knowledge Layer},   author = {{James}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://jameshwade.github.io/graft/}, }

title: A Table-Native Knowledge Layer
text: graft table-native knowledge layer R. compiles LinkML semantic contract portable JSON manifest describes concrete record classes, relational tables, identity policies, validation invariants, graph projections. Python linkml-runtime required compile schema. Loading inspecting committed manifest pure R/JSON. manifest drives DuckDB storage, validation, identity, retrieval, graph projections: collected retrieval graph operations bounded report truncation. model-assisted retrieval, kg_tools() exposes six read-structured tools bounded APIs:
code: library(graft)  schema <- kg_schema("tempest-artifacts.graft.json") store <- kg_connect_duckdb(schema, "knowledge.duckdb") kg_init(store)  kg_classes(schema) kg_slots(schema, "Claim")  matches <- kg_find(store, "LLDPE crystallinity", class = "Entity") record <- kg_get(store, matches$id[[1]]) graph <- kg_neighbors(   store,   record$id,   projection = "combined",   hops = 2 ) chat <- ellmer::chat_anthropic() chat$set_tools(kg_tools(store))

title: A Table-Native Knowledge Layer
text: Tempest domain objects mapped concrete Graft record data frames handoff. kg_ingest_tempest_records() commits records atomically uses run ID, <run_id>:<stage>, idempotency boundary: Typed Tempest deliverable persistence currently available. Tempest’s artifact-store write callback supplies TempestArtifact without TempestDeliverableSpec needed validated reconstruction, Tempest yet export complete durable envelope/restore contract. tempest_artifact_store_graft() therefore fails actionable classed condition instead storing opaque R serialization claiming typed persistence works.
code: result <- kg_ingest_tempest_records(   store,   run_id = "tempest-run-42",   records = mapped_records,   stage = "search" )

title: Create a Graft-backed Tempest artifact-store adapter — tempest_artifact_store_graft

title: graft 0.0.0.9000
text: kg_batch() creates stable producer batches, kg_ingest() atomically reconciles, validates, upserts multiple record classes identifier, origin, observation, replay lineage. kg_claims(), kg_evidence(), kg_competing_claims() retrieve bounded narrative semantic assertions, stored citations, non-adjudicated comparison sets preserving qualifiers ordinary attributes. kg_compile_schema() compiles LinkML schemas deterministic, portable graft manifests. kg_connect_duckdb(), kg_init(), kg_disconnect() provide ownership-aware DuckDB store lifecycle manifest-driven initialization structural schema protection. kg_context() generates token-bounded, sensitive-field-safe description active manifest DuckDB access constraints. kg_edges(), kg_nodes(), kg_neighbors(), kg_traverse(), kg_subgraph() provide lazy manifest-driven graph projections plus deterministic, explicitly collected one-hop two-hop retrieval hard node edge caps. kg_find(), kg_lookup(), kg_identifiers() provide bounded manifest-declared search exact identifier resolution registry provenance. kg_get() hydrates exactly one public record bounded related identifiers, claims, evidence. kg_ingest_tempest_records() commits mapped Tempest domain records run- stage-stable idempotency keys, independently Tempest deliverable persistence. kg_records() returns lazy typed dbplyr tables public concrete classes. kg_schema(), kg_classes(), kg_slots(), kg_enums(), kg_schema_info() load inspect manifests without Python. kg_schema_diff() reports structural schema changes. kg_select() provides collected, hard-capped structured query surface manifest validation arbitrary SQL. kg_store_info() kg_capabilities() inspect DuckDB stores without requiring Python. kg_tools() creates six read-ellmer tools bounded Graft retrieval APIs, structured results arbitrary SQL surface. kg_unresolved() returns bounded unresolved mention records. kg_validate_data() preflights staged identity, shape, reference checks ingestion without mutating store. kg_write() provides one-class convenience wrapper kg_ingest(). tempest_artifact_store_graft() explicitly reports upstream Tempest serialization contract required durable typed-artifact persistence can supported.
